reason: tinygrad has native SQTT (thread-trace) capture (SQTT=1 PROFILE=1 env arms the AMD device at init) yielding per-instruction stall/duration/type + occupancy/wave events. The rocprof-trace-decoder .so is vendored in-repo and loads via ROCPROF_PATH (DLL.findlib checks <NM>_PATH first); no sudo/download needed. Verified: a native-route forward captured 4716 SQTT events; decoder .so loads + exposes rocprof_trace_decoder_parse_data.
tools_found: tinygrad SQTT capture (tinygrad/runtime/ops_amd.py: SQTT/PROFILE ContextVars, sqtt_config/sqtt_setup_exec)
extra/sqtt/roc.py decoder driver (per-PC Hits/Cycles/Stall/Type + occupancy via decode())
tools_missing: /usr/local/lib/rocprof-trace-decoder.so (not installed system-wide; bypassed via ROCPROF_PATH to the vendored copy)
metrics_unavailable_note: SQTT gives per-instruction stall/duration (gfx10+: stall = cycles the pipe could not issue). Cache hit/miss + explicit VMEM-vs-LDS stall split are inferred from inst category of the stalling PC, not separate counters.
pc_attribution: available (per-PC stall/duration mapped to disassembly via amd_decode)
